title: Sysmon Event IDs
overview: | Event ID | Description | | --- | --- | | 1 | Process creation | | 2 | File creation time changes (defense evasion aka time stomping) | | 3 | Network connections | | 4 | Sysmon service state change | | 5 | Process termination | | 6 | Driver loaded | | 7 | Image loaded | | 8 | Create Remote Thread detected | | 9 | Raw disk access detected | | 10 | Process ac

study.overview: | Event ID | Description | | --- | --- | | 1 | Process creation | | 2 | File creation time changes (defense evasion aka time stomping) | | 3 | Network connections | | 4 | Sysmon service state change | | 5 | Process termination | | 6 | Driver loaded | | 7 | Image loaded | | 8 | Create Remote Thread detected | | 9 | Raw disk access detected | | 10 | Process ac
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
